keys.debug.selfProfiling.dialog.text:
The IDE will start a new ETW profiling session in a separate process.
It will capture sampling information about debugger processes that are running or will be launched after the session starts.

To start an ETW session, Windows will prompt for administrative privileges.

When finished, stop the profiling session using the controls in the status bar.
Do not terminate the ETW process via Windows Task Manager:
The ETW session persists beyond the application's lifetime, and a Windows reboot will be required to stop the session.

Projects using Daemon toolchain allow builds to automatically detect 
  installed toolchains given the defined JVM criteria or download a compatible one if cannot be found locally. In addition, using 
  the Daemon toolchain aligns the selection between CLI and IDE, avoiding spawning multiple Daemons improving performance but also 
  makes it simple to handle the required toolchain on different machines.

keys.gradle.cmd.option.full.stacktrace: Print out the full (very verbose) stacktrace for all exceptions.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.stacktrace: Print out the stacktrace for all exceptions.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.scan: Creates a build scan. Gradle will emit a warning if the build scan plugin has not been applied.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.no.scan: Disables the creation of a build scan.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.build.cache: Enables the Gradle build cache. Gradle will try to reuse outputs from previous builds.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.configuration.cache: Enables the configuration cache. Gradle will try to reuse the build configuration from previous builds.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.configuration.cache.pr: Configures how the configuration cache handles problems (fail or warn). Defaults to fail.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.configure.on.demand: Configure necessary projects only. Gradle will attempt to reduce configuration time for large multi-project builds.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.no.configure.on.demand: Disables the use of configuration on demand.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.max.workers: Configure the number of concurrent workers Gradle is allowed to use.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.parallel: Build projects in parallel. Gradle will attempt to determine the optimal number of executor threads to use.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.no.parallel: Disables parallel execution to build projects.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.priority: Specifies the scheduling priority for the Gradle daemon and all processes launched by it. Values are 'normal' (default) or 'low'.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.profile: Profile build execution time and generates a report in the <build_dir>/reports/profile directory.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.watch.fs: Enables watching the file system for changes, allowing data about the file system to be re-used for the next build.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.debug: Log in debug mode (includes normal stacktrace).
keys.gradle.cmd.option.console: Specifies which type of console output to generate. Values are 'plain', 'auto' (default), 'rich' or 'verbose'.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.warning.mode: Specifies which mode of warnings to generate. Values are 'all', 'fail', 'summary'(default) or 'none'.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.include.build: Include the specified build in the composite.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.offline: Execute the build without accessing network resources.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.dry.run: Run the builds with all task actions disabled.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.write.locks: Persists dependency resolution for locked configurations, ignoring existing locking information if it exists.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.update.locks: Perform a partial update of the dependency lock, letting passed in module notations change version.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.gradle.user.home: Specifies the gradle user home directory.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.project.dir: Specifies the start directory for Gradle. Defaults to current directory.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.project.cache.dir: Specify the project-specific cache directory. Defaults to .gradle in the root project directory.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.system.prop: Set system property of the JVM (e.g. -Dmyprop=myvalue).
keys.gradle.cmd.option.project.prop: Set project property for the build script (e.g. -Pmyprop=myvalue).
keys.gradle.cmd.option.exclude.task: Specify a task to be excluded from execution.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.continue: Continue task execution after a task failure.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.continuous: Continuous Build allows you to automatically re-execute the requested tasks when task inputs change.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.export.keys: Exports the public keys used for dependency verification.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.refresh.keys: Refresh the public keys used for dependency verification.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.dependency.verificatio: Configures the dependency verification mode (strict, lenient or off)
keys.gradle.cmd.option.write.verification.met: Generates checksums for dependencies used in the project (comma-separated list).
keys.gradle.cmd.option.daemon: Uses the Gradle Daemon to run the build. Starts the Daemon if not running.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.no.daemon: Do not use the Gradle daemon to run the build. Useful occasionally if you have configured Gradle to always run with the daemon by default.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.status: Shows status of running and recently stopped Gradle Daemon(s).
keys.gradle.cmd.option.stop: Stops the Gradle Daemon if it is running.
keys.gradle.cmd.option.foreground: Starts the Gradle Daemon in the foreground.

keys.notification.gradle.legacy.firstLoad.inf:
The Gradle configuration phase needs to be run to get the Script Configuration. Loading the script configuration is disabled by default, since it can be resource-intensive for large Gradle projects.

Click "Load Configuration" to evaluate the Gradle Kotlin DSL script.

Alternatively, to automatically load the configuration when the script first opens and reload it each time build script configuration blocks are changed, you can enable "auto-reload" for all scripts. This option is not recommended for large Gradle projects with long configuration times.
keys.notification.gradle.legacy.outsideProjec: Code insight unavailable (related Gradle project not linked).

The Gradle project that evaluates this script needs to be imported to have it analyzed by the IDE. Try re-importing the linked Gradle project or link a new Gradle project that evaluates this script.

Alternatively, you can add it to standalone scripts, and its configuration will be loaded automatically.

NOTE: Each standalone script requires a separate Gradle configuration phase to be executed on update. This can be resource intensive for large Gradle projects.

keys.warning.duplicate.predefined.character.c: Duplicate predefined character class ''{0}'' inside character class

Default values are represented using an annotation attribute prefix:defaultValue where prefix is bound to http://relaxng.org/ns/compatibility/annotations/1.0 as defined by the RELAX NG DTD Compatibility Committee Specification. By default, Trang will use a for prefix unless that conflicts with a prefix used in the DTD.
Preserves the exact semantics of ANY content models by using an explicit choice of references to all declared elements. By default, Trang uses a wildcard that allows any element.

Replaces colons in element names by chars when constructing the names of definitions used to represent the element declarations and attribute list declarations in the DTD. Trang generates a definition for each element declaration and attlist declaration in the DTD. The name of the definition is based on the name of the element. In RELAX NG, the names of definitions cannot contain colons. However, in the DTD, the element name may contain a colon. By default, Trang will first try to use the element names without prefixes. If this causes a conflict, it will instead replace the colon by a legal name character (it try first to use a period).

Specifies how to construct the name of the definition representing an element declaration from the name of the element. The name-pattern must contain exactly one percent character. This percent character is replaced by the name of element (after colon replacement) and the result is used as the name of the definition

Specifies not to generate definitions for attribute list declarations and instead move attributes declared in attribute list declarations into the definitions generated for element declarations. This is the default behavior when the output module is xsd. Otherwise, the default behaviour is as described in the -i no-inline-attlist parameter.
Specifies the name of the definition generated for the content of elements declared in the DTD as having a content model of ANY.

Specifies whether Trang should generate a start element. DTDs do not indicate what elements are allowed as document elements. Trang assumes that all elements that are defined but never referenced are allowed as document elements.

This specifies how to construct the name of the definition representing an attribute list declaration from the name of the element. The name-pattern must contain exactly one percent character. This percent character is replaced by the name of element (after colon replacement) and the result is used as the name of the definition.
Specifies the value for the processContents attribute of any Attribute \nelements. The default is skip (corresponding to RELAX NG semantics).

Specifies the value for the processContents attribute of any elements. 
The default is skip (corresponding to RELAX NG semantics) unless the 
input format is dtd, in which case the default is strict (corresponding to 
DTD semantics).
Disables the use of abstract elements and subsitution groups \nin the generated XML Schema. This can also be controlled using \nan annotation attribute.

keys.sml.studiobot.onboarding.aida.simplified: Use Gemini chat to assist in app development
Transform selected code in the editor
The Privacy Notice shown below and Google\u2019s [Privacy Policy](https://policies.google.com/privacy) describe how Gemini handles your data.

When you use Gemini in {0}, Google collects chat text, prompts, related code, generated output, related feature usage information, and your feedback. Google uses this data to provide, improve, and develop Google products and services and machine learning technologies, including Google’s enterprise products such as Google Cloud.

To help with quality and improve our products, human reviewers may read, annotate, and process your prompts, related code, generated output, related feature usage information, and your feedback. Your data will be retained for up to 18 months and stored in a way where Google cannot tell who provided it. Google cannot fulfill deletion requests during this time.

**Do not include sensitive (e.g., confidential) or personal information that can be used to identify you or others in your prompts or feedback.**

Using project context will help generate code that is more specific to your codebase and project. Confirm what setting you\u2019d like to start with. These can be changed at any time in **Settings > Tools > {0}**.

Allow project context use to get full access to all Gemini features.\n\nYou can also use an .aiexclude file in your project to exclude specific files and directories. [Learn more](https://d.android.com/r/studio-ui/gemini/data-and-privacy/aiexclude)
Use chat only, without project context

Don't allow project context use and get limited access to Gemini features. Gemini will only use your prompts and past conversation history to respond. Some features, such as AI code completion, will not be available in this mode.

* Save frequently-used prompts in the prompt library and access them via `@`\n
* Select code in the editor and press `{0}` to prompt Gemini inline

keys.aiplugin.timeline.emptyState.sampleQuery: Implement unit tests for my code
Tell me how to use Gemini Code Assist

Gemini Code Assist can help you do the following:

* Generate code and tests for your project
* Provide guidance on what you're trying to do with your code
* Use context from your local codebase to provide informed answers in chat
* Explain the code to you
* Show code suggestions and completions
* Find bugs and suggest fixes

For more information on how to use Gemini in your IDE, see:

* [Write code in IntelliJ with Gemini assistance](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/ui/write-code)
* [See the Codelab](https://codelabs.developers.google.com/codelabs/cloud-developer-duetai?hl=en#0)

There are 3 types of validation rules:

  • {regexp#integer} or {regexp:-?\\d+} checks that the value is integer.
